description: Letter from Manṣūr (not Manṣūr b. Sālim) in Alexandria to his brother in Fustat mentioning that his son Moshe started to speak and has since kept repeating: 'my uncle, my uncle Musa,' referring to his deceased uncle and the sender's and recipient's brother, who bore the same name. The sender also writes about an agreement that was reached with a tax-farmer about a payment made in installments and expresses regret about the fact that a female relative who was involved in a law-suit with her husband enjoyed 'no holiday and no Saturday.' Finally, the writer requests the recipient urgently to return a note from the tax-farmer. The Verso is written in a different hand. (Information from Mediterranean Society, III, p. 235, and from Goitein's index cards)
description_he: מכתב ממנצור באלכסנדריה לאחיו בפסטאט, מדווח שבנו משה החל לדבר וחוזר ואומר: 'דודי, דודי מוסא', הכוונה לדודו המנוח בעל אותו שם. גם דיווח על הסכם עם גבאי מס ודאגה לקרובה הנמצאת בסכסוך עם בעלה 'ללא חג ושבת'.
